notes: Replaced bootstrap placeholder with a task-local child result.
Runtime session id is not exposed in the current Codex environment; evidence is anchored through named source paths and line references.

claim_context.summary: No active task claims were present at analysis time; repo claim registry returned zero active claims.

evidence: C45 already treats model execution as a bounded substrate choice inside a sovereign execution wrapper rather than as a peer architectural layer.
C47 already owns the long-term independent native model lane, so the future problem is promotion and substrate adoption, not inventing a second model-serving doctrine.
C36 blocks any move that turns the idea into a new external ingress plane.
C39 already owns delegated sampling semantics, so a new inference protocol family would be duplication.
C18 and C22 already model compute, provider dependence, and rollout burden as program-level planning surfaces.
T-300 forbids reviving old-stack architectural shapes as forward authority.

evidence_paths.relevance: C45 already owns route execution posture, native-versus-leased substrate split, provider non-equivalence, and membrane-bounded leased cognition.
C42 already owns priming, runtime handoff, and anti-ambient-authority constraints; a generic inference bus would violate that posture.
C47 already defines the staged sovereignty roadmap and the native model adaptation lane that replaces provider dependence over time.
C36 is the canonical external boundary and explicitly rejects generic gateway thinking, so the idea cannot become a new externally legible service plane.
C39 already covers delegated sampling and keeps message semantics distinct from transport or service-topology invention.
C18 already prices sovereign compute, leased cognition, and Forge promotion as first-order economic realities, which points toward a planning frame instead of a new revenue-neutral layer.
C22 already acknowledges bounded leased/native model operations, but treats implementation by specification boundary rather than by reviving a standalone inference microservice architecture.
T-300 fixes Alternative C as forward authority and requires replacement rather than reinterpretation of old-stack architectural frames.

lane_strategy: Challenge topology, authority boundaries, or core primitives instead of merely optimizing the current frame.

model_audit.routing_basis: AAS4 ordinary FULL_PIPELINE / IDEATION default future-branch routing target.

objective: Challenge the current architectural frame itself and explore a replacement future for the current ideation problem. Apply that branch specifically to 'Independent Model Inference Microservice Layer'.
one_sentence_thesis: The real missing answer is not an independent inference microservice layer at all, but a cross-spec sovereign cognition substrate planning frame that binds route selection, execution priming, model promotion, capacity classes, and compute economics without creating a second service plane.

option_shapes.name: Sovereign Cognition Substrate Planning Frame
option_shapes.summary: Replace the candidate subsystem with a cross-spec planning frame that defines execution substrate classes, route-to-substrate binding, promotion handoff, capacity quotas, and cost triggers across C45, C47, C18, and C22.
option_shapes.main_upside: Solves the real scale-separation problem without violating current authority boundaries.
option_shapes.main_risk: Cross-spec scope can blur ownership unless the frame is tightly partitioned into direct-spec obligations.

option_shapes.name: Internal Inference Utility Plane
option_shapes.summary: If a new module is still desired, narrow it to an internal-only placement, batching, caching, and quota control plane beneath C45 and downstream of C47.
option_shapes.main_upside: Creates an explicit operational substrate while remaining inside the membrane and protocol boundaries.
option_shapes.main_risk: Still risks duplicating C45, C42, and C47 unless the authority seam is defined with unusual precision.

option_shapes.name: Reject As Already Good Enough
option_shapes.summary: Treat the current architecture as sufficient and solve any residual pain through scattered direct-spec clarifications only.
option_shapes.main_risk: Leaves substrate orchestration implicit and pushes a real capacity-management problem back into ad hoc implementation.

recommended_next_action: Do not promote 'Independent Model Inference Microservice Layer' as a new standalone FULL PIPELINE invention. Replace it first with a cross-spec planning frame for sovereign cognition substrate management, and only mint a new subsystem later if that planning pass reveals a genuinely independent authority seam that cannot live inside C45/C47/C18/C22.

authority_surface_readout.disposition: Keep as the route-level authority; do not create a peer inference layer above it.
Treat expensive model execution as lease-visible priming and handoff, not as ambient substrate access.
Keep long-term model independence in the Forge promotion lane rather than splitting it into a second doctrine.
Any viable answer must remain internal and membrane-bounded.
Model the idea as compute-planning and cost-allocation work, not as a free-standing neutral service.
Sequence it as spec-boundary planning and rollout change, not as revival of a literal microservice layer.
Reuse existing sampling and stream semantics; do not invent a new inference protocol family.
Reject verbatim restoration of old-stack framing as forward authority.

key_upsides: Preserves the real objective: separating lightweight agent behavior from heavy model-capacity management.
Aligns the idea with the existing sovereignty roadmap instead of fighting it.
Converts an architecture collision into an explicit planning and cost-discipline surface.
key_risks: A planning-frame answer can feel less tangible than a new subsystem and may be under-scoped by default.
If the operator actually needs live placement and batching authority, direct-spec work may still expose a later need for a real module.
Historical microservice language in C22 can tempt an invalid old-stack interpretation if T-300 is not enforced aggressively.
